div1.entryFree:
Beggar, vb. 1) to reduce to beggary: lean, rent and --ed by the strumpet wind, Merch. II, 6, 19. big Mars seems bankrupt in their --ed host, H5 IV, 2, 43. it (conscience) --s any man that keeps it, R3 I, 4, 145. bowed you to the grave and --ed yours for ever, Mcb. III, 1, 91. Metaphorically: b. the estimation which you prized richer than sea and land, Troil. II, 2, 91. it --ed all description, Ant. II, 2, 203.
2) followed by of, == to deprive, to make destitute of: now nature bankrupt is, --ed of blood, Sonn. 67, 10. necessity, of matter --ed, will nothing stick our person to arraign, Hml. IV, 5, 92.
